author: NumberFour8
body:
The Connector sync process must use an Open channel filter, because the Graph subscription feeds only opened channels. Before the fix, it could lead to inability to sync the node, because closed channels were counted, but not fed via the subscription.

It also makes percentage of total sync configurable.

Needs https://github.com/hoprnet/blokli/issues/218 to be fixed, in order to function.
title: fix: use Opened channel filter on Connector sync

author: Teebor-Choka
body:
This pull request refines the HOPR node startup sequence by introducing more granular state transitions and improving logging for each initialization step. The changes enhance the ability to track node progress in detail, which benefits UI feedback and debugging. Additionally, there are dependency updates and minor code cleanups.

**Node Initialization State Improvements**

* Expanded the `HoprState` enum in `hopr/hopr-lib/src/state.rs` to include detailed, step-by-step states for the node lifecycle, each with descriptive strings suitable for UI display. The enum now covers all major initialization phases, such as waiting for funds, checking balance, validating network configuration, subscribing to announcements, registering Safe, announcing the node, awaiting key binding, initializing services, running, and terminated.
* Updated `hopr/hopr-lib/src/lib.rs` to set the new granular states at the appropriate points during node startup, ensuring accurate progress tracking. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-dd3e00deb7558c2678c6984b11fabc2f69ac770270c874cdcc65be5487cbfbf4R351) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-dd3e00deb7558c2678c6984b11fabc2f69ac770270c874cdcc65be5487cbfbf4L363-R364) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-dd3e00deb7558c2678c6984b11fabc2f69ac770270c874cdcc65be5487cbfbf4R382-R383) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-dd3e00deb7558c2678c6984b11fabc2f69ac770270c874cdcc65be5487cbfbf4R443-R445) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-dd3e00deb7558c2678c6984b11fabc2f69ac770270c874cdcc65be5487cbfbf4R477) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-dd3e00deb7558c2678c6984b11fabc2f69ac770270c874cdcc65be5487cbfbf4R517-R518) [[7]](diffhunk://#diff-dd3e00deb7558c2678c6984b11fabc2f69ac770270c874cdcc65be5487cbfbf4R544-R545) [[8]](diffhunk://#diff-dd3e00deb7558c2678c6984b11fabc2f69ac770270c874cdcc65be5487cbfbf4R560-R562)

**Logging and Observability Enhancements**

* Added informative log messages throughout the node initialization process in `hopr/hopr-lib/src/lib.rs`, making it easier to follow progress and diagnose issues. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-dd3e00deb7558c2678c6984b11fabc2f69ac770270c874cdcc65be5487cbfbf4R657) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-dd3e00deb7558c2678c6984b11fabc2f69ac770270c874cdcc65be5487cbfbf4R679) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-dd3e00deb7558c2678c6984b11fabc2f69ac770270c874cdcc65be5487cbfbf4R725)

**Dependency Updates**

* Updated dependencies in `Cargo.toml`: bumped `bytes` from `1.11.0` to `1.11.1`

**Code Cleanup**

* Removed the unused `Measurement` enum from `hopr/api/src/network/types.rs` to streamline the codebase.
title: feat(hopr-lib): add more state granularity

The issue was fixed by moving the tracking code to take care of both local and non-local connections.

Fixes #7732

author: NumberFour8
body:
The new `hoprd-gen-test` can be used to generate HOPR identities, Safes and hoprd configuration files given a Blokli instance.

This is useful for creating local `hoprd` cluster:

```shell
docker run --rm -p 8080:8080 -e RUST_LOG=debug bloklid-anvil:latest
cargo run --bin hoprd-gen-test
```

This will generate deterministic identity files, deploy and fund their Safes and generate `hoprd` configuration files in `/tmp/hopr-nodes/` using the local Blokli instance inside the Docker container.

Refs #7672
title: feat: add `hoprd-gen-test` node test deployment generator

body:
Fixes #218 

 - Add event-bus–driven subscriptions for channel and transaction updates, replacing polling with `async-broadcast`
 - Add `status` filter to the `channels` query for filtering by channel lifecycle state
 - Randomize Phase 1 subscription results
 - Introduce `account_current` and `channel_current` database views and migrate all state queries to use them, eliminating manual `ORDER BY / LIMIT 1` patterns
 - Refactor model conversions where possible

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Two-phase subscriptions: randomized initial snapshots + real-time filtered updates.
  * Event-driven transaction status subscriptions (subscribe to status deltas).
  * Channel status filtering in queries and subscriptions.

* **Improvements**
  * DB-side current-state views for faster, consistent reads.
  * Batch account loading to avoid N+1 lookups.
  * SSE stream events labeled as "next".

* **Documentation**
  * Expanded architecture, transaction store, batch-loading, and Phase 1 ordering guidance.

* **Tests**
  * Expanded integration and subscription tests for Phase 1/2 and transaction sequencing.

* **Chores**

author: tolbrino
body:
Fixes #198

The idea is to find the modules for each pre-seeded safe at startup instead of relying on the indexer alone. Thus the startup has been extended with 2 steps:

(1) Pre-seed safe data always (no-op if it was pre-seeded before)
(2) Then for each pre-seeded safe which has not additional state entry yet the on-chain data will be read to get the module address

In order to implement these changes the safe contracts table has been extended to use the same temporal setup like the accounts table.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * CSV-based pre-seeding of safes with a startup refresh that updates pre-seeded safe modules and returns simple update/unchanged/error counts.
  * New startup module and a DB accessor to help integrations interact with the indexer.

* **Chores**
  * Database schema bumped to version 11 with a temporal current-state model for safe contracts and accompanying migration.
  * Crate version bumps across multiple packages and migration wiring updates.

<sub>✏️ Tip: You can customize this high-level summary in your review settings.</sub>
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
title: feat(db): Turn safe contracts into a temporal table set
